title: HTML Header & Footer: Structuring Top and Bottom Sections

meta.title: HTML Header & Footer – Layout, Usage & Best Practices (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to use header and footer elements to structure web pages with navigation, branding, and bottom sections.

content.value: The <header> and <footer> elements are semantic HTML tags used to define the top and bottom sections of a webpage or a section.
The <header> typically contains branding, logos, navigation links, or introductory content.
The <footer> typically contains copyright information, contact details, and additional links.

My Site

Welcome to my website

© 2026 My Site
You can use multiple header and footer elements within sections or articles.

value: **Avoid overloading footer with too much content**
**Use semantic structure for better SEO and accessibility**

content.value: HTML header and footer elements are essential for structuring web pages. Proper use improves readability, SEO, and accessibility.

faq.question: What is the header tag?
faq.answer: It defines the top section of a webpage or section.

faq.question: What is the footer tag?
faq.answer: It defines the bottom section of a webpage or section.

faq.question: Can I use multiple headers?
faq.answer: Yes, within different sections or articles.

quiz.question: Which tag defines the top section?
Which tag defines the bottom section?
Which tag is used for navigation?
